prompt:
You are a Senior Business Analyst and Automation Architect responsible for translating business process requirements into a structured automation workflow.

Your task is to interpret the business process specification below and design a workflow that correctly implements the described process.

The workflow must be logically correct, complete, and capable of executing autonomously without human intervention.

Do not simplify the process and do not omit any rule.

The workflow must preserve the operational logic and business rules defined in the specification.

⸻

WORKFLOW DESIGN METHOD

Before generating the workflow, you must internally identify the following elements:
	1.	Source systems
	2.	Collections that require iteration
	3.	The fundamental processing unit
	4.	Required data evaluation and classification logic
	5.	Conditional rules
	6.	Output destinations
	7.	Exception handling paths

Collections must always be processed using iteration logic.

Decision rules must be represented using conditional branches.

Grouping logic must be implemented when results must be aggregated by a specific field.

⸻

PROCESS OBJECTIVE

Analyze lead records stored in Google Sheets, identify high-quality leads based on a score threshold, group them by the responsible sales person, and generate targeted follow-up messages for each sales person containing their high-quality leads.

⸻

SOURCE SYSTEM

The workflow reads lead data from Google Sheets.

Google Sheet ID:

1LKhXUzV9xh-q1NZJKHDjJWPdwFHXalV6amwLJwX8JkE

The sheet tab containing the lead data may be specified by the user.

If the tab name is not provided, the workflow must request the tab name before continuing.

Execution guidance:

The rows of the sheet must be treated as a collection.

Each row represents a single lead record.

⸻

LEAD DATA STRUCTURE

Each lead row contains the following fields that must be used for processing and reporting:

Date
Lead Name
Company
Email
Phone
Stage
Notes
Sales Person

These fields must remain associated with each lead throughout the workflow.

⸻

LEAD SCORE FIELD

The workflow must identify the column that contains the numeric lead score used for qualification.

If the score column is not provided by the user, the workflow must ask the user to identify the score column.

Execution guidance:

The lead score must be read for each lead record and used for classification logic.

⸻

PROCESSING UNIT

The primary processing unit for the workflow is a lead record (one row in the sheet).

Execution guidance:

The workflow must iterate through all lead rows individually.

⸻

LEAD CLASSIFICATION

For each lead row, read the lead score from the score column.

A lead is classified as a high-quality lead if:

Lead Score >= User-provided threshold value

Execution guidance:

This rule must be implemented using conditional logic.

⸻

HIGH QUALITY LEAD GROUPING

All high-quality leads must be grouped by the value in the "Sales Person" field.

Execution guidance:

This grouping is required so that each sales person receives a follow-up message containing only their leads.

⸻

SALES PERSON EMAIL RESOLUTION

The Sales Person field may contain either:

An email address
A name

If the Sales Person field contains an email address, that email address must be used as the recipient of the follow-up email.

If the Sales Person field contains a name instead of an email address, the workflow must resolve the email address using user-provided mapping rules.

Execution guidance:

The mapping rules will translate sales person names into email addresses.

⸻

UNRESOLVED SALES PERSON

If a lead is classified as high-quality but the sales person email cannot be resolved:

The lead must still be included in the summary table.

The lead details must be emailed to:

avital.livovsky@gmail.com

Execution guidance:

This rule must be handled as an exception branch.

⸻

FOLLOW-UP MESSAGE GENERATION

For each sales person group, generate a short sales-friendly follow-up message.

The message must include:

The list of high-quality leads assigned to that sales person

Key context from the following fields:

Stage
Notes

A suggested next step for each lead.

Execution guidance:

The follow-up message should summarize the leads clearly and concisely to help the sales person take action quickly.

⸻

EMAIL DELIVERY

Send one email per sales person containing their high-quality leads and follow-up message.

Execution guidance:

Each email must include only the leads assigned to that specific sales person.

⸻

SYSTEM INTEGRATIONS

The workflow interacts with the following systems:

Google Sheets (lead data source)

Email system (sending follow-up messages)

⸻

DATA TRACEABILITY REQUIREMENT

Each lead included in a follow-up email must remain traceable to its original row in the Google Sheet.

The workflow must preserve the lead data fields throughout the processing pipeline.

⸻

WORKFLOW EXECUTION REQUIREMENTS

The workflow must correctly support:

Reading multiple lead rows

Iterating through lead records

Evaluating lead scores

Classifying high-quality leads

Grouping leads by sales person

Resolving sales person email addresses

Handling unresolved email exceptions

Generating targeted follow-up messages

Sending grouped follow-up emails

⸻

FINAL TASK

Using the above business process specification, generate a workflow that fully implements the described process.

The workflow must include all necessary steps, iterations, grouping operations, and decision points required to execute the process correctly.

The workflow must automate the entire process end-to-end.
